Definition: Entire lifecycle of waste: generation, collection, transport, recovery and disposal, including supervision of such operations.
Proactive and systematic monitoring, control, and optimization of energy consumption to conserve use and decrease energy costs.
Monitoring, reporting, and reduction of GHG emissions and air pollutants such as NOx, SOx, VOC, PM that affect human health and the environment.
Effective oversight of water withdrawal, consumption and discharge to minimise environmental impact and reduce water-related risks.
Systems and processes to address ESG and operational challenges in the supply chain from sourcing materials to delivering final products.
Organisation efforts to interact, collaborate, and build relationships with communities to create positive social impact and address community concerns.
Fostering a workforce reflecting varied backgrounds and creating an inclusive environment where all employees have equal opportunities.
Fundamental rights and freedoms for all individuals, eliminating child and forced labour, harassment and discrimination throughout operations and supply chains.
Practices and procedures to safeguard data from unauthorised access, breaches, and compliance with data privacy laws and regulations.
Implementing a safe and healthy work environment, preventing workplace hazards, promoting employee well-being, and complying with health and safety regulations.
Ensuring products meet defined standards of reliability, performance and safety, preventing defects or hazards that could pose risks to users.
Commitment to conducting business with integrity and openness, focusing on ethical behaviour, corporate governance, and anti-competitive conduct.
Adherence to national and international legal, regulatory, and industry-specific standards governing the organisation operations.
Designing and manufacturing products that minimise environmental and social impacts across their lifecycle from raw material sourcing to disposal.
Entity economic value creation and distribution, reflecting overall economic impact on society as employer, market participant, and value chain actor.
Strategy addressing how operations affect natural habitats, species, and ecosystems particularly in sensitive or high-biodiversity areas.
Practices for managing customer interactions to enhance satisfaction, loyalty and trust through transparent communication and effective complaint handling.
Systematic process of investing, implementing and developing new products, processes or technologies to improve efficiency and competitive advantage.
Strategies to retain skilled employees, reduce turnover, foster positive workplace culture, and provide professional development opportunities.
Processes for selecting, engaging, and overseeing suppliers to meet operational needs and ESG criteria including sustainability and ethical governance.
All employees and workers in upstream and downstream value chain who can be materially impacted by the organisation including living wages and health and safety.
